# typed: false

class Vote < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :story
  belongs_to :comment, optional: true

  normalizes :reason, with: ->(r) { r.to_s }, apply_to_nil: true

  # for comment_vote_summaries
  attribute :count, :integer
  attribute :usernames, :string

  validates :vote, presence: true, inclusion: {in: [1, -1]}
  validates :reason,
    length: {is: 1},
    allow_blank: true,
    presence: true
  validate :validate_flagger_hasnt_replied

  scope :comments_flags, ->(comments, user = nil) {
    q = where(comment: comments, vote: -1)
    user ? q.where(user: user) : q.all
  }

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  COMMENT_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "M" => "Me-too",
    "T" => "Troll",
    "U" => "Unkind",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_COMMENT_REASONS = COMMENT_REASONS.merge({
    "I" => "Incorrect"
  }).freeze

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  STORY_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "A" => "Already Posted",
    "B" => "Broken Link",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_STORY_REASONS = STORY_REASONS.merge({
    "Q" => "Low Quality"
  }).freeze

  def on_comment?
    comment_id.present?
  end

  def on_story?
    comment_id.blank?
  end

  def reason_text
    if on_story?
      ALL_STORY_REASONS[reason]
    else
      ALL_COMMENT_REASONS[reason]
    end
  end

  def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)
    Vote
      .joins(:user)
      .select("comment_id, reason, count(1) as count, group_concat(username separator ', ') as usernames")
      .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
      .where.not(reason: "")
      .group(:comment_id, :reason)
      .group_by(&:comment_id)
  end

  def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)
    votes = {}

    Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,
      comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
    votes = {}

    Vote.where(
      user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id
    ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
    if story_ids.empty?
      {}
    else
      votes = where(
        user_id: user_id,
        comment_id: nil,
        story_id: story_ids
      )
      votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
        memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
      end
    end
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
    return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?

    votes = where(
      user_id: user_id,
      comment_id: comment_ids
    ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
      memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end
  end

  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
  )
    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize

    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
    # happen with associated comments.
    v.story if story_id.present?

    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change

    # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
    # no vote  -> 1      1
    # flag -> 1          1
    # no vote  -> flag   0
    # flag -> no vote    0
    # 1  -> no vote     -1
    # 1  -> flag        -1
    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
      1
    elsif v.vote == 1
      -1
    else
      0
    end
    flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
      -1
    elsif new_vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
      1
    else
      # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
      0
    end

    if new_vote == 0
      v.destroy!
    else
      v.vote = new_vote
      v.reason = reason
      v.save!
    end

    if update_counters
      t = v.target
      if v.user_id != t.user_id
        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta
      end
      t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    end
  end

  def target
    if comment_id
      Comment.find(comment_id)
    else
      Story.find(story_id)
    end
  end

  def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
    if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
      errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
    end
  end
end

class Vote < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :story
  belongs_to :comment, optional: true

  normalizes :reason, with: ->(r) { r.to_s }, apply_to_nil: true

  # for comment_vote_summaries
  attribute :count, :integer
  attribute :usernames, :string

  validates :vote, presence: true, inclusion: {in: [1, -1]}
  validates :reason,
    length: {is: 1},
    allow_blank: true,
    presence: true
  validate :validate_flagger_hasnt_replied

  scope :comments_flags, ->(comments, user = nil) {
    q = where(comment: comments, vote: -1)
    user ? q.where(user: user) : q.all
  }

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  COMMENT_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "M" => "Me-too",
    "T" => "Troll",
    "U" => "Unkind",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_COMMENT_REASONS = COMMENT_REASONS.merge({
    "I" => "Incorrect"

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  STORY_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "A" => "Already Posted",
    "B" => "Broken Link",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_STORY_REASONS = STORY_REASONS.merge({
    "Q" => "Low Quality"

  def on_comment?
    comment_id.present?
  end

  def on_story?
    comment_id.blank?
  end

  def reason_text

      ALL_STORY_REASONS[reason]
    else
      ALL_COMMENT_REASONS[reason]
    end
  end

  def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)

      .joins(:user)
      .select("comment_id, reason, count(1) as count, group_concat(username separator ', ') as usernames")
      .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
      .where.not(reason: "")
      .group(:comment_id, :reason)

  def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)

    Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,
      comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|

    end

    votes
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
    votes = {}

    Vote.where(
      user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id
    ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
    if story_ids.empty?
      {}
    else
      votes = where(
        user_id: user_id,
        comment_id: nil,
        story_id: story_ids
      )
      votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
        memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
      end
    end
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
    return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?

    votes = where(
      user_id: user_id,
      comment_id: comment_ids
    ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
      memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end
  end

  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
  )
    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize

    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
    # happen with associated comments.
    v.story if story_id.present?

    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change

    # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
    # no vote  -> 1      1
    # flag -> 1          1
    # no vote  -> flag   0
    # flag -> no vote    0
    # 1  -> no vote     -1
    # 1  -> flag        -1
    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
      1
    elsif v.vote == 1
      -1
    else
      0
    end
    flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
      -1
    elsif new_vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
      1
    else
      # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
      0
    end

    if new_vote == 0
      v.destroy!
    else
      v.vote = new_vote
      v.reason = reason
      v.save!
    end

    if update_counters
      t = v.target
      if v.user_id != t.user_id
        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta
      end
      t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    end
  end

    if comment_id
      Comment.find(comment_id)
    else
      Story.find(story_id)
    end
  end

  def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
    if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
      errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
    end
  end
end

337	  # TODO: race condition: if two votes arrive at the same time, the second one
338	  # won't take the first's score change into effect for calculated_confidence
339	  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
340	    self.score += score_delta
341	    self.flags += flag_delta
342	    new_confidence = calculated_confidence
343	    # confidence_order allows sorting sibling comments by confidence in queries like story_threads.
344	    # confidence_order must sort in ascending order so that it's in the right order when
345	    # concatenated into confidence_order_path, which the database sorts lexiographically. It is 3
346	    # bytes wide. The first two bytes map confidence to a big-endian unsigned integer, inverted so
347	    # that high-confidence have low values. confidence is based on the number of upvotes and flags,
348	    # so some values (like the one for 1 vote, 0 flags) are very common, causing sibling comments to
349	    # tie. If we don't specify a tiebreaker, the database will return results in an arbitrary order,
350	    # which means sibling comments will swap positions on page reloads (infrequently and
351	    # intermittently, real fun to debug). So the third byte is the low byte of the comment id. Being
352	    # assigned sequentially, mostly the tiebreaker sorts earlier comments sooner. We average ~200
353	    # comments per weekday so seeing rollover between sibling comments is rare. Importantly, even
354	    # when it is 'wrong', it gives a stable sort.
355	    Comment.connection.execute <<~SQL
356	      UPDATE comments SET
357	        score = (select coalesce(sum(vote), 0) from votes where comment_id = comments.id),
358	        flags = (select count(*) from votes where comment_id = comments.id and vote = -1),
359	        confidence = #{new_confidence},
360	        confidence_order = concat(lpad(char(65535 - floor(#{new_confidence} * 65535) using binary), 2, '\0'), char(id & 0xff using binary))
361	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
362	    SQL
363	  end
364	
365	  def gone_text
366	    if is_moderated?
367	      "Comment removed by moderator " <<
368	        moderation.try(:moderator).try(:username).to_s << ": " <<
369	        (moderation.try(:reason) || "No reason given")
370	    elsif user.is_banned?
371	      "Comment from banned user removed"
372	    else
373	      "Comment removed by author"
374	    end

file.content:
end

  # TODO: race condition: if two votes arrive at the same time, the second one
  # won't take the first's score change into effect for calculated_confidence
  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    self.score += score_delta
    self.flags += flag_delta
    new_confidence = calculated_confidence
    # confidence_order allows sorting sibling comments by confidence in queries like story_threads.
    # confidence_order must sort in ascending order so that it's in the right order when
    # concatenated into confidence_order_path, which the database sorts lexiographically. It is 3
    # bytes wide. The first two bytes map confidence to a big-endian unsigned integer, inverted so
    # that high-confidence have low values. confidence is based on the number of upvotes and flags,
    # so some values (like the one for 1 vote, 0 flags) are very common, causing sibling comments to
    # tie. If we don't specify a tiebreaker, the database will return results in an arbitrary order,
    # which means sibling comments will swap positions on page reloads (infrequently and
    # intermittently, real fun to debug). So the third byte is the low byte of the comment id. Being
    # assigned sequentially, mostly the tiebreaker sorts earlier comments sooner. We average ~200
    # comments per weekday so seeing rollover between sibling comments is rare. Importantly, even
    # when it is 'wrong', it gives a stable sort.
    Comment.connection.execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE comments SET
        score = (select coalesce(sum(vote), 0) from votes where comment_id = comments.id),
        flags = (select count(*) from votes where comment_id = comments.id and vote = -1),
        confidence = #{new_confidence},
        confidence_order = concat(lpad(char(65535 - floor(#{new_confidence} * 65535) using binary), 2, '\0'), char(id & 0xff using binary))
      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
    SQL
  end

  def gone_text
    if is_moderated?
      "Comment removed by moderator " <<
        moderation.try(:moderator).try(:username).to_s << ": " <<
        (moderation.try(:reason) || "No reason given")
    elsif user.is_banned?
      "Comment from banned user removed"
    else
      "Comment removed by author"
    end

file.content:
Markdowner.to_html(description, allow_images: can_have_images?, as_of: created_at)
  end

  # TODO: race condition: if two votes arrive at the same time, the second one
  # won't take the first's score change into effect for calculated_hotness
  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    self.score += score_delta
    self.flags += flag_delta
    Story.connection.execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE stories SET
        score = (
          select sum(vote) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and (
            (vote = 1) or
            (
              vote = -1 and
              not exists ( -- user didn't comment
                select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
              )
            )
          )
        ),
        flags = (select count(*) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and
          vote = -1 and
          not exists ( -- user didn't comment
            select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
          )
        ),
        hotness = #{calculated_hotness}
      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
    SQL
  end

  def has_suggestions?
    suggested_taggings.any? || suggested_titles.any?
  end

  def header_anchor
    "header_#{token}"
  end

  # calling .count on a preloaded association still triggers a count query
  def hider_count
    @hider_count ||= hidings.to_a.size
  end

5:describe Vote do
6:  it "is not valid without a vote amount" do
15:  context "upvoting a story and flags" do
21:    it "-1 to 1" do
22:      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, s.id, nil, u.id, "S")
26:      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(1, s.id, nil, u.id, nil)
32:    it "-1 to 0" do
33:      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, s.id, nil, u.id, "S")

406	
407	    it "deducts from score when users flag" do
408	      expect(story.score).to eq(1) # from submitter's upvote
409	      flagger = create(:user)
410	      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, story.id, nil, flagger.id, "S")
411	      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(0)
412	      expect(story.reload.flags).to eq(1)
413	    end
414

416	      expect(story.score).to eq(1) # from submitter's upvote
417	      flagger = create(:user)
418	      create(:comment, story: story, user: flagger)
419	      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, story.id, nil, flagger.id, "S")
420	      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(1)
421	      expect(story.reload.flags).to eq(0)
422	    end

427	      HiddenStory.hide_story_for_user(story, hider)
428	      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(1)

file.content:
describe "update_score_and_recalculate" do
    let(:story) { create(:story) }

    it "deducts from score when users flag" do
      expect(story.score).to eq(1) # from submitter's upvote
      flagger = create(:user)
      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, story.id, nil, flagger.id, "S")
      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(0)
      expect(story.reload.flags).to eq(1)
    end

    it "doesn't deduct from score if flagging user commented" do
      expect(story.score).to eq(1) # from submitter's upvote
      flagger = create(:user)
      create(:comment, story: story, user: flagger)
      Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(-1, story.id, nil, flagger.id, "S")
      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(1)
      expect(story.reload.flags).to eq(0)
    end

    it "doesn't deduct from score if hiding user didn't flag" do
      expect(story.score).to eq(1) # from submitter's upvote
      hider = create(:user)
      HiddenStory.hide_story_for_user(story, hider)
      expect(story.reload.score).to eq(1)

145	  describe "Comment#update_score_and_recalculate matches SQL math" do
146	    it "initializes correctly" do
147	      c = create(:comment, id: 9, score: 1, flags: 0)
148	      c.reload
149	      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes).to eq([159, 30, c.id])
150	    end
151	
152	    it "increments correctly" do
153	      c = create(:comment, id: 4, score: 1, flags: 0)
154	      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes).to eq([0, 0, 0]) # placeholder on creation
155	      create(:vote, story: c.story, comment: c)
156	      c.update_score_and_recalculate!(1, 0)
157	      c.reload
158	      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes.last).to eq(c.id) # id included after vote
159	    end
160	  end
161	end
162

file.content:
describe "Comment#update_score_and_recalculate matches SQL math" do
    it "initializes correctly" do
      c = create(:comment, id: 9, score: 1, flags: 0)
      c.reload
      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes).to eq([159, 30, c.id])
    end

    it "increments correctly" do
      c = create(:comment, id: 4, score: 1, flags: 0)
      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes).to eq([0, 0, 0]) # placeholder on creation
      create(:vote, story: c.story, comment: c)
      c.update_score_and_recalculate!(1, 0)
      c.reload
      expect(c.confidence_order.bytes.last).to eq(c.id) # id included after vote
    end
  end
end

```json
    "enclosing_method": "Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because (app/models/vote.rb:121), the shared entry point every cast vote calls",
    "target_obtained_at": "app/models/vote.rb:177 — t = v.target",
    "target_definition": "Vote#target at app/models/vote.rb:185-191 — returns Comment.find(comment_id) when comment_id is present, else Story.find(story_id)",
    "note": "update_counters for author karma happens just before, at app/models/vote.rb:178-180, also keyed on t.user_id"
  },
  "step2_what_it_recomputes_and_persists": {
    "polymorphic": "update_score_and_recalculate! has two implementations dispatched by the target's class",
    "story": {
      "method": "Story#update_score_and_recalculate! at app/models/story.rb:693-718",
      "in_memory": "increments self.score and self.flags (story.rb:694-695)",
      "columns_written": ["score", "flags", "hotness"],
      "sql": "raw UPDATE stories ... (story.rb:696-717): score = sum(vote) over story votes excluding flags from users who commented; flags = count of such flags; hotness = computed value",
      "value_method": "calculated_hotness, defined at app/models/story.rb:550 (interpolated at story.rb:715)"
    },
    "comment": {
      "method": "Comment#update_score_and_recalculate! at app/models/comment.rb:339-363",
      "in_memory": "increments self.score and self.flags (comment.rb:340-341)",
      "columns_written": ["score", "flags", "confidence", "confidence_order"],
      "sql": "raw UPDATE comments ... (comment.rb:355-362): score = coalesce(sum(vote),0); flags = count of vote=-1; confidence = computed value; confidence_order = packed 3-byte sort key (2 bytes from confidence, 1 byte = id & 0xff tiebreaker)",
      "value_method": "calculated_confidence, defined at app/models/comment.rb:232 (interpolated at comment.rb:359 and comment.rb:360)"
    },
    "shared_caveat": "Both carry a documented race condition (comment.rb:337-338, story.rb:691-692): concurrent votes don't see each other's delta when computing confidence/hotness"
  },
  "step3_change_map": {
    "files_to_edit": [
      "app/models/story.rb:693-718 — Story#update_score_and_recalculate!",
      "app/models/comment.rb:339-363 — Comment#update_score_and_recalculate!"
    ],
    "supporting_value_methods_if_touched": [
      "app/models/story.rb:550 — calculated_hotness",
      "app/models/comment.rb:232 — calculated_confidence"
    ],
    "single_call_site_to_preserve": "app/models/vote.rb:181 (signature update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)); target resolution at vote.rb:177/185",
    "other_callers_to_keep_working": [
      "app/models/comment.rb:484 — update_score_and_recalculate!(0, 0) (trigger db calc)",
      "app/models/comment.rb:543 — update_score_and_recalculate!(0, 0) (restore score for sorting)",
      "app/models/hidden_story.rb:15 and :21 — story.update_score_and_recalculate!(0, 0)"
    ],
    "specs_that_must_cover_the_recalculation": [
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82 — score/flag deltas across all vote transitions (-1/0/1) on stories",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:93-176 — story upvote+karma, no-op on existing upvote, hide vote, comment flag/karma neutralization",
      "spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-422 — 'update_score_and_recalculate' group: flags deduct from score, exemption when flagger commented",
      "spec/models/story_spec.rb:373-399 — #calculated_hotness values",
      "spec/extras/bitpacking_spec.rb:145-160 — Comment#update_score_and_recalculate confidence_order byte packing matches SQL math",
      "spec/models/comment_spec.rb:228-239 — calculated_confidence behavior",
      "spec/models/comment_spec.rb:141,188,201,215 and spec/requests/comments_spec.rb:45 — votes driving comment score/flag recalculation"
    ],
    "regression_risk": "Changing the SQL aggregation (the flagger-commented exemption in story.rb:703-705/711-713) or the confidence_order packing (comment.rb:360) is what the above specs guard; run vote_spec, story_spec, comment_spec, and bitpacking_spec together"
  }
}
```
